questions.text: Which phylum includes sponges?
questions.explanation: Sponges belong to the phylum Porifera.

questions.text: Animals with radial symmetry include:
questions.explanation: Radial symmetry is seen in cnidarians and echinoderms.

questions.text: A true coelom is present in which animal group?
questions.explanation: Annelids have a well-developed coelom.

questions.text: Which feature is characteristic of arthropods?
questions.explanation: Arthropods have a hard external skeleton called an exoskeleton.

questions.text: Vertebrates are distinguished by the presence of a:
questions.explanation: Vertebrates have a backbone made of vertebrae.

questions.text: Which animal group uses a muscular foot for movement?
questions.explanation: Molluscs like snails and squids use a muscular foot for locomotion.

questions.text: When any plane passing through the central axis of the body divides the organism into two identical halves, it is called:
questions.explanation: Coelenterates, ctenophores and echinoderms have this kind of body plan.

questions.text: Animals in which the cells are arranged in two embryonic layers, an external ectoderm and an internal endoderm, are called:
questions.explanation: An undifferentiated layer, mesoglea, is present in between the ectoderm and the endoderm in coelenterates.

questions.text: In some animals, the body cavity is not lined by mesoderm, instead, the mesoderm is present as scattered pouches. Such a body cavity is called:
questions.explanation: Aschelminthes are pseudocoelomates.

questions.text: The phenomenon where the body is externally and internally divided into segments with a serial repetition of at least some organs is called:
questions.explanation: In earthworm, the body shows this pattern and the phenomenon is known as metamerism.

questions.text: The notochord is derived from:
questions.explanation: It is a mesodermally derived rod-like structure formed on the dorsal side during embryonic development.

questions.text: Water enters through minute pores (ostia) into a central cavity (spongocoel), from where it goes out through the osculum in:
questions.explanation: Sycon belongs to Phylum Porifera (sponges) which have a canal system.

questions.text: The specialized cells that line the spongocoel and the canals in sponges are called:
questions.explanation: Choanocytes (collar cells) help in water circulation and food capture.

questions.text: Cnidarians exhibit two basic body forms called:
questions.explanation: Polyp is sessile and cylindrical, while Medusa is umbrella-shaped and free-swimming.

questions.text: The phenomenon of alternation of generation seen in Obelia is called:
questions.explanation: Polyps produce medusae asexually and medusae form the polyps sexually.

questions.text: Ctenophores are commonly known as:
questions.explanation: They possess eight external rows of ciliated comb plates and are exclusively marine.

questions.text: The property of a living organism to emit light is called:
questions.explanation: Bioluminescence is well-marked in ctenophores.

questions.text: Platyhelminthes are commonly called flatworms because:
options: They have a flat ventral side
questions.explanation: They are mostly endoparasites found in animals including human beings.

questions.text: Specialized cells in flatworms that help in osmoregulation and excretion are:
questions.explanation: Flame cells are characteristic of Phylum Platyhelminthes.

questions.text: The body of Aschelminthes is circular in cross-section, hence the name:
questions.explanation: They may be free-living, aquatic and terrestrial or parasitic in plants and animals.

questions.text: Annelids possess longitudinal and circular muscles which help in:
questions.explanation: Aquatic annelids like Nereis possess lateral appendages, parapodia, which help in swimming.

questions.text: In Annelida, excretion and osmoregulation are performed by:
questions.explanation: Nephridia help in the removal of metabolic wastes.

questions.text: The largest phylum of Animalia which includes insects is:
questions.explanation: Over two-thirds of all named species on earth are arthropods.

questions.text: The respiratory organs in arthropods include:
questions.explanation: Arthropods show a variety of respiratory adaptations for aquatic and terrestrial life.

questions.text: Sensory organs like antennae, eyes, and balancing organs (statocysts) are present in:
questions.explanation: Arthropods have well-developed sensory systems.

questions.text: The second largest animal phylum is:
questions.explanation: Molluscs are terrestrial or aquatic having an organ-system level of organisation.

questions.text: A file-like rasping organ for feeding found in the mouth of molluscs is called:
questions.explanation: Radula is used to scrape food particles.

questions.text: The most distinctive feature of echinoderms is the presence of a:
questions.explanation: The water vascular system helps in locomotion, capture and transport of food and respiration.

questions.text: Hemichordates have a rudimentary structure in the collar region called:
questions.explanation: Stomochord is a structure similar to notochord.

questions.text: Phylum Chordata is characterized by the presence of:
questions.explanation: These are the fundamental characteristics of chordates.

questions.text: In Urochordata, the notochord is present only in:
questions.explanation: In Cephalochordata, it extends from head to tail region and is persistent throughout life.

questions.text: All living members of the class Cyclostomata are:
questions.explanation: They have an elongated body bearing 6-15 pairs of gill slits for respiration.

questions.text: Chondrichthyes are marine animals with a ________ endoskeleton.
questions.explanation: Their mouth is located ventrally and the skin is tough, containing minute placoid scales.

questions.text: Osteichthyes includes both marine and fresh water fishes with a ________ endoskeleton.
questions.explanation: They have four pairs of gills which are covered by an operculum on each side.

questions.text: Amphibians can live in aquatic as well as terrestrial habitats. Most of them have:
questions.explanation: The body is divisible into head and trunk. Tail may be present in some.

questions.text: Reptiles are characterized by:
questions.explanation: The skin has epidermal scales or scutes.

questions.text: In Aves (birds), the forelimbs are modified into:
questions.explanation: Hind limbs generally have scales and are modified for walking, swimming or clasping.

questions.text: The most unique mammalian characteristic is the presence of:
questions.explanation: Mammary glands are used to nourish the young ones.

questions.text: Animals that are cold-blooded (cannot regulate their body temperature) are called:
questions.explanation: Amphibians, reptiles and fishes are poikilothermic.

questions.text: Birds and mammals are:
questions.explanation: Homeothermic animals are able to maintain a constant body temperature.
